user_query:
I’m organizing my neighbourhood association’s notes into a concise trivia-focused digest. Please do the following using only the files in input/ and produce the specified outputs:

Inputs:
- input/agenda_items.csv
- input/notes_2024-03.md
- input/notes_2024-04.md
- input/existing_summary.md

What I need:
1) Filter agenda items to Montreal political trivia:
   - Read input/agenda_items.csv (columns: id,title,topic,scope,priority; where priority is an integer and 1 means highest priority).
   - Keep only rows where topic == "political_trivia" AND scope == "Montreal".

2) Count mentions from meeting notes and compute a score:
   - Read input/notes_2024-03.md and input/notes_2024-04.md.
   - For each kept agenda item id (e.g., A5), count the total occurrences of the exact token "[A#]" across both notes files (e.g., count "[A5]" occurrences; headings and body text both count).
   - For each item, compute score = (mentions * 2) + (6 - priority).
   - Sorting: sort primarily by score (desc), then by mentions (desc), then by title (asc).

3) Produce a ranked CSV:
   - Save to outputs/trivia_ranked.csv with header: id,title,priority,mentions,score.
   - Include only the filtered Montreal political trivia items with their computed counts and score.
   - Ensure that items with scope != "Montreal" (e.g., A3 in the inputs) are excluded even if they have many mentions.

4) Update the existing summary document:
   - Open input/existing_summary.md and create outputs/meeting_summary_updated.md.
   - In the updated file, preserve all content outside the markers exactly as-is.
   - Replace the content between the markers
     <!-- START TRIVIA -->
     ...
     <!-- END TRIVIA -->
     with a fresh section that:
     - Begins with a single line title like: "Top 3 Montreal Political Trivia (Mar–Apr 2024)".
     - Lists the top 3 items from outputs/trivia_ranked.csv in ranked order (1 to 3). For each item, include "Title (ID)" followed by up to two concise factual bullet points extracted from the notes.
       • Extraction rule: scan notes in chronological order (notes_2024-03.md, then notes_2024-04.md). For each item id, under any heading line that contains the id (e.g., a line with "[A5]"), collect up to the first two lines starting with "- " that follow that heading before the next heading. Use those lines (lightly edit for clarity and sentence case if needed).
     - End the section with a short source line: "Sources: notes_2024-03.md, notes_2024-04.md; ranked via agenda_items.csv."

Deliverables:
- outputs/trivia_ranked.csv (ranked, filtered items with id,title,priority,mentions,score)
- outputs/meeting_summary_updated.md (identical to input/existing_summary.md except the content between the trivia markers is replaced as specified)

Notes:
- Do not add or remove any other sections in the summary. Only replace the content strictly between the markers.
- The filtering, counting, ranking, and rewriting should be fully reproducible from the provided inputs. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
